---
title: "Survey Questions for Erikson"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-03"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Assignment: Measuring Psychosocial Development in College Students
Individual work: write 3 survey questions
Group work: review/vote on questions to be included in a survey (this will be a different assignment)



---

### Purpose

This assignment builds your skill in operationalizing psychological theory. You will apply **Erik Erikson’s psychosocial theory** by writing survey questions that could measure where college students are within the relevant developmental stage.

---

### Background

According to Erik Erikson, traditional college-age individuals (approximately 18-25) are typically navigating from the stage of **Identity vs. Role Confusion** and, for some, into the beginning of **Intimacy vs. Isolation**. 


---

### Your Task

Create **three (3) original survey questions** designed to assess college students’ development in the Eriksonian stage most relevant to traditional college-age adults.

You must:

1. Choose an Erikson stage you believe could apply to college students

   * Industry vs. Inferiority
   * Identity vs. Role Confusion
   * Intimacy vs. Isolation

2. Write **three survey questions** that could measure that stage
3. Use clear, neutral wording suitable for a college survey
4. Use the same response format for all three questions (recommended: 7-point Likert scale)

---

### Required Response Format

Use this scale for each item:

> 1 = Strongly disagree
> 2 = Disagree
> 3 = Disagree somewhat
> 4 = Neutral
> 5 = Agree somewhat
> 6 = Agree
> 7 = Strongly agree

---

### Submission Components

**Part A: Stage Selection (2–3 sentences)**

* Name the Erikson stage you chose
* Briefly justify why it fits college students

**Part B: Survey Items (your main work)**
Provide **three original items**.

Example format (do not copy these):

* I have a clear sense of who I am and what I value.
* I feel uncertain about my future direction.
* I am confident in the career path I am choosing.

**Part C: Brief Rationale (3–5 sentences)**
Explain how your items reflect the psychological features of the chosen stage.

---

### Quality Guidelines (Read Carefully)

Strong items are:

* Specific and concrete
* Focused on one idea at a time
* Neutral in tone (not leading)
* Appropriate for college populations
* Clearly connected to Erikson’s theory

Avoid:

* Double-barreled questions (two ideas in one item)
* Vague wording (e.g., “things,” “stuff,” “often”)
* Yes/no questions
* Questions about childhood (focus on current functioning)

---

### Grading Criteria (10 points total)

* Stage correctly identified and justified (2 pts)
* Three well-written survey items (4 pts)
* Clear connection to Erikson’s theory (3 pts)
* Professional formatting and clarity (1 pt)

---
